question: When is the Financial Forecast Committee meeting scheduled?
expected_answer: The meeting is scheduled for December 9, 2025 at 6:00pm in Conference Room A on the 3rd Floor of Beverly City Hall.
source_title: Financial Forecast Committee Meeting

question: What will be discussed at the Financial Forecast Committee meeting?
expected_answer: The agenda includes opening comments by the chairperson and general committee discussion, approval of minutes, approval of the final draft report, closing commentary, and adjournment.
source_title: Financial Forecast Committee Meeting

question: When is the next Historic District Commission meeting in Beverly?
expected_answer: Wednesday, April 29, 2026 at 7:15 PM at City Council Chambers, Beverly City Hall, 191 Cabot Street, Beverly MA 01915. This is an in-person meeting.
source_title: Historic Districts Commission Meeting

question: What is being discussed about Chapter 168 at the Historic District Commission meeting?
expected_answer: Suggested updates to Chapter 168 Historic Buildings and Districts, including the City's Demolition Delay procedures, will be presented and discussed as a Citizen Presentation & Discussion.
source_title: Historic Districts Commission Meeting

question: When is the next Harbor Management Authority meeting?
expected_answer: The next meeting is April 15, 2026 at 6:45 pm at the Beverly Police Station Community Room or via Google Meet.
source_title: Harbor Management Authority Meeting

question: What improvements are planned for Glover Wharf?
expected_answer: Planned improvements include installing a dinghy bar and additional fishing-related and dinghy tie-up signage, adding security gates to the commercial float, upgrading electricity on the recreational float, and installing a webcam on the transient float.
source_title: Harbor Management Authority Meeting

question: When is the next Beverly Retirement Board meeting?
expected_answer: The next Beverly Retirement Board meeting is scheduled for April 30, 2026 at 6:00 P.M. at 275 Rantoul Street, Beverly, MA.

question: Who is the Executive Director of the Beverly Retirement Board?
expected_answer: Adam Travinski is the Executive Director of the Beverly Retirement Board.

question: When is the next Stormwater Committee meeting in Beverly?
expected_answer: Tuesday, November 26, 2024 from 10AM to 11AM at City Council Chambers, City Hall, 191 Cabot Street.

question: What topics will be discussed at the Stormwater Committee meeting?
expected_answer: The meeting will cover program status updates including ordinances and regulations, dry and wet weather updates, IDDE sampling, city asset inspections and maintenance, private development inspections, LID guidelines, GIS development, Year 7 objectives, and budget.

expected_answer: Adam Travinski is the Executive Director of the Beverly Retirement Board.

question: Will City Hall be open on Monday April 20th?
expected_answer: No, City Hall and all City buildings (excluding public safety) will be closed on Monday, April 20, 2026 for Patriots' Day observance. They will reopen on Tuesday, April 21 during normal business hours.
source_title: Patriots' Day Observance

question: When is my trash pickup this week?
expected_answer: All trash and recycling routes will be delayed by one day this week due to the Patriots' Day holiday.
source_title: Patriots' Day Observance

question: What is the Beverly Housing Rehabilitation Program and when will the city request funding for it?
expected_answer: The Beverly Housing Rehabilitation Program (BHRP) is a program that will be funded through Massachusetts Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) Program Community Development Fund 2025 funds. The City of Beverly, through the Department of Planning & Development, will request the release of these funds on or about May 1, 2026.
source_title: Notice of Intent to Request Release of Funds

question: Where can I find more information about this project and how do I submit public comments?
expected_answer: More information is available on www.beverlyma.gov/CDBG, and an Environmental Review Record is on file at the Beverly Planning & Development Department at 191 Cabot Street during business hours. Public comments are being accepted through April 25, 2026.
source_title: Notice of Intent to Request Release of Funds

question: Who is the contractor installing the new water meters in Beverly?
expected_answer: The contractor is Hydro Utilities. You will receive an official letter from them with instructions and program details when they work in your area.
source_title: Info For City Wide Water Meter Change-Out Program

question: Will I have to pay for the new water meter installation?
expected_answer: No, the meter and reading device are supplied and installed at no cost to the water customer.
source_title: Info For City Wide Water Meter Change-Out Program

question: When was the City Council project update about City Hall renovation released?
expected_answer: The project update was released on January 20, 2026, with the announcement dated January 22, 2026.
source_title: Beverly City Hall Renovation and Expansion  City Council Project Update  January 20, 2026

question: What is the Beverly City Hall project about?
expected_answer: The project involves renovation and expansion of Beverly City Hall, with updates being provided by the City Council.
source_title: Beverly City Hall Renovation and Expansion  City Council Project Update  January 20, 2026

question: Did Beverly update its immigration policy for police?
expected_answer: Yes, Police Chief LeLacheur updated the Beverly Police Department Immigration Status and Detainer Policy in 2026, building on the previous 2017 policy.
source_title: Joint Proclamation: Immigration and the Values of the City of Beverly

question: Who signed the joint proclamation on immigration in Beverly?
expected_answer: Mayor Mike Cahill, City Council President Flowers, and School Committee President Visnick issued the joint proclamation, and they invited City Council and School Committee colleagues to sign on.
source_title: Joint Proclamation: Immigration and the Values of the City of Beverly

question: Why was the Public Works Administration unit restructured for FY24?
expected_answer: The unit was restructured to consolidate various positions from the Highway Division and Engineering Department into a single unit for central support administrators and staff, allowing this unit to better administer and coordinate among all other Department of Public Service Units and Divisions. There was no overall FTE impact from this restructuring.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2025 Proposed Operating Budget — Page 206

question: Why is there a new Public Works Administration unit in the FY24 budget?
expected_answer: It's a newly restructured unit that consolidates central support administrators and staff from the Highway Division and Engineering Department into a single unit for better coordination across all Public Services divisions. There is no change to the total number of positions.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2024 Proposed Operating Budget — Page 205

question: What departments are included in Beverly's Public Safety section?
expected_answer: The Public Safety section includes six departments: Animal Control, Police Department, Fire Department, Public Safety Dispatch, Emergency Management, and Harbormaster.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2025 Proposed Operating Budget — Page 165

question: What is the projected wage increase for teachers in Beverly's FY2026 budget?
expected_answer: The projected wage increase for Unit A (Teachers) is 6.8% based on FY'25 staffing levels.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2026 Proposed Operating Budget — Page 8

question: What percentage of the Beverly Public Schools budget goes to salaries and benefits?
expected_answer: Salaries, benefits, and other compensation account for 83.4% of the total Beverly Public Schools Budget in FY'26.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2026 Proposed Operating Budget

question: What are the main goals for Beverly Public Schools in FY2026?
expected_answer: The three core goals are: fostering a sense of belonging for every student and staff member, empowering educators through meaningful professional growth opportunities, and upholding a commitment to innovative practices and the highest educational standards.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2026 Proposed Operating Budget — Page 6

question: Why did the FY2024 budget increase so much compared to last year?
expected_answer: The budget increased by $6,944,500 primarily due to non-discretionary spending requirements and modest revenue increases. The city addressed the initial imbalance by refining requested funding, pulling some capital investments into FY2023, and relying on steady revenue streams to cover cost increases.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2024 Proposed Operating Budget — Page 21

question: How much is the General Fund budget increasing in FY2025?
expected_answer: The General Fund budget is proposed to increase by 5.38% over the FY2024 budget.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2025 Proposed Operating Budget

question: Why does Beverly's budget use different accounting methods than regular GAAP accounting?
expected_answer: Beverly uses budgetary basis accounting in accordance with Massachusetts state law and Department of Revenue (DOR) accounting practices. This method is required for the city's annual budget and property tax certification process, even though it departs from generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) in several ways.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2025 Proposed Operating Budget — Page 17

question: What is the Fire Department budget for FY2025 and how does it compare to last year?
expected_answer: The FY2025 Fire Department budget is $7,965,853, which represents a decrease of $298,309 or 3.61% compared to the prior year (FY2024).
source_title: Beverly MA FY2025 Proposed Operating Budget — Page 178

question: How much is the Fire Department budget increasing for FY2024?
expected_answer: The Fire Department budget is increasing by $327,910, which represents a 4.13% increase compared to the prior year.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2024 Proposed Operating Budget — Page 171

question: How far ahead does Beverly's capital plan look?
expected_answer: The plan covers a five-year period as required by the Beverly City Charter, but also includes projects that may need attention within the next ten years to support long-term financial and service planning.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2024 Proposed Operating Budget — Page 302

question: How far ahead does Beverly's capital plan look?
expected_answer: The plan covers at least five years as required by the Beverly City Charter, but also includes projects that may need attention within the next ten years to support long-term financial and service planning.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2025 Proposed Operating Budget — Page 308

question: How much money is being transferred for road and sidewalk work in FY25?
expected_answer: The proposed budget includes $1.25M transfer to a paving account for road and sidewalk work. This will be combined with other resources like State Chapter 90 funds and grant funding.
source_title: Beverly MA FY2025 Proposed Operating Budget — Page 289
